description: The shell executes simple echo commands from stdin in sequence.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo hello\necho world\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: echo prints arguments with spaces and echo -n suppresses the trailing newline.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo a b c\necho -n tail\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(repr(p.stdout))

description: Quoted strings preserve spaces while unquoted whitespace separates arguments.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
cmd = "echo 'one two' three\necho \"four five\" six\n"
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input=cmd, text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())
expected_output:
one two three
four five six

description: cd changes the working directory and pwd reports the updated directory.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    os.mkdir(os.path.join(td, 'sub'))
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='pwd\ncd sub\npwd\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    lines = p.stdout.strip().splitlines()
    print(os.path.basename(lines[-1]))
    print(lines[0] != lines[-1])

description: export sets an environment variable and later echo expands it.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='export NAME=alice\necho $NAME\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: unset removes an environment variable and undefined variables expand to an empty string.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='export NAME=alice\nunset NAME\necho before-$NAME-after\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: env prints only managed shell variables in deterministic order.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='export B=2\nexport A=1\nenv\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: A simple pipeline passes stdout from the left command to the right command.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo hello | cat\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: Output redirection writes to a file and input redirection reads from that file.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo hello > out.txt\ncat < out.txt\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: $? expands to the latest command exit status after a successful command.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo ok\necho $?\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: exit terminates command processing and later commands are not executed.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo before\nexit\necho after\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: Unknown commands report diagnostics on stderr while later valid commands still write clean stdout.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='unknown_cmd\necho after\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print('stdout=' + p.stdout.strip())
print('stderr_has_error=' + str(bool(p.stderr.strip())))

description: cat reads a named file and writes its contents to stdout.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'data.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('one\ntwo\n')
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='cat data.txt\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: Pipeline and redirection characters inside quotes are treated as literal text.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo "a | b"\necho \'x > y\'\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: Working-directory state persists across multiple commands and affects later pwd output.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    os.makedirs(os.path.join(td, 'a', 'b'))
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='cd a\npwd\ncd b\npwd\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    lines = [os.path.basename(x) for x in p.stdout.strip().splitlines()]
    print(lines)

description: An exported variable expands before command execution and its output flows through a pipeline.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='export WORD=hello\necho $WORD | cat\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: Single quotes disable variable expansion while double quotes allow it in the same process.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
cmd = "export X=abc\necho '$X'\necho \"$X\"\n"
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input=cmd, text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: Builtin output redirected to a file can later be read and passed through a pipeline.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo alpha > data.txt\ncat < data.txt | cat\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: The order of replacement and append redirections determines final file contents.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo one > out.txt\necho two >> out.txt\necho reset > out.txt\necho three >> out.txt\ncat out.txt\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: A failed cd does not change the current working directory and later valid commands still work.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    os.mkdir(os.path.join(td, 'ok'))
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='cd ok\ncd missing_dir\npwd\necho still-running\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    lines = p.stdout.strip().splitlines()
    print(os.path.basename(lines[-2]))
    print(lines[-1])

description: A failed export does not corrupt previously defined environment variables.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='export GOOD=ok\nexport 1BAD=broken\necho $GOOD\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip().splitlines()[-1])

description: Pipeline output and pipeline exit status are handled consistently.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo hello | grep h\necho $?\necho hello | grep z\necho $?\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: unset updates both later variable expansion and environment listing.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='export TEMPVAR=gone\nunset TEMPVAR\necho x$TEMPVAR y\nenv\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
out = p.stdout
print(out.splitlines()[0])
print('TEMPVAR=gone' in out)

description: Redirection scope and pipeline dataflow remain separate when combined in one command line.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo hidden > file.txt | cat\necho visible\ncat file.txt\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())

description: A failed command reports on stderr, updates $? to a nonzero value, and allows later commands to continue with clean stdout.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='unknown_cmd\necho $?\necho after\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
lines = p.stdout.strip().splitlines()
print(lines[0].isdigit())
print(lines[0] != '0')
print(lines[1])
print(bool(p.stderr.strip()))

description: An unmatched quote reports an error on stderr and later input lines still execute.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
cmd = "echo before\necho 'broken\necho after\n"
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input=cmd, text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print('stdout=' + p.stdout.strip())
print('stderr=' + str(bool(p.stderr.strip())))

description: A grep miss in a simple pipeline sets the latest status to 1 for later $? expansion.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess
root = os.getcwd()
p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input='echo hello | grep z\necho $?\n', text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=root)
print(p.stdout.strip())

description: Input redirection on a later pipeline stage overrides that stage input without leaking the earlier pipe data.
test_code:
import os, sys, subprocess, tempfile
root = os.getcwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'left.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('left\n')
    with open(os.path.join(td, 'right.txt'), 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        f.write('right\n')
    cmd = 'cat left.txt | cat < right.txt\necho $?\n'
    p = subprocess.run([sys.executable, os.path.join(root, 'main.py')], input=cmd, text=True, capture_output=True, cwd=td)
    print(p.stdout.strip())
